tasks.description: Fix CompactPreview slider crash (bugs #12/#9). File: src/tools/video-editor/components/CompactPreview.tsx:80 and src/shared/components/ui/slider.tsx:31. The Slider wrapper (slider.tsx:31) passes base-ui's raw value through: `(value) => onValueChange(value)`. When `value` prop is `[currentTime]` (array), base-ui's `onValueChange` receives `number[]`. The wrapper's TypeScript signature declares `(value: number) => void` but passes `number[]` at runtime. CompactPreview:80 destructures `([value])` expecting array. The crash likely happens when base-ui passes a scalar `number` (for single-thumb sliders some versions do this). FIX: (1) In slider.tsx:31, change to `(value) => onValueChange(Array.isArray(value) ? value[0] : value)` to always unwrap to scalar. (2) In CompactPreview:80, change `onValueChange={([value]) => ...}` to `onValueChange={(value) => previewRef.current?.seek(value)}` to match the wrapper's scalar API. (3) Verify the slider is now interactive (clickable + draggable) in the compact editor pane.
tasks.executor_notes: Normalized the shared Slider wrapper to a scalar callback, updated the compact playhead consumer, and aligned the clip property sliders so the video-editor slider API is consistent at runtime. Build and focused tests passed, which catches the crash path and the wrapper contract change.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/components/ui/slider.test.tsx src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts

tasks.description: Add acknowledgeRemotionLicense prop to Player (bug #13). File: src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.tsx:95-109. Add `acknowledgeRemotionLicense` prop to the `<Player />` component. Check `@remotion/player` types for exact prop name — likely `acknowledgeRemotionLicense={true}`. This suppresses the console license warning.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the installed @remotion/player package exposes acknowledgeRemotionLicense and passed it through RemotionPreview so both the compact pane and full preview stop emitting the warning. The project build completed cleanly with the prop in place.

tasks.description: Fix unused track count to exclude default V1/A1 (bug #7). File: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:224-231. Import DEFAULT_VIDEO_TRACKS from '@/tools/video-editor/lib/defaults'. In the `unusedTrackCount` memo (line 224-231), add: `const defaultTrackIds = new Set(DEFAULT_VIDEO_TRACKS.map(t => t.id));` then filter: `return resolvedConfig.tracks.filter(track => !tracksWithClips.has(track.id) && !defaultTrackIds.has(track.id)).length;`. Also verify `handleClearUnusedTracks` (line 233-261) preserves V1/A1 explicitly — current logic keeps one empty per kind as fallback, which effectively keeps V1/A1 since they're first, but add explicit guard: `if (defaultTrackIds.has(track.id)) return true;` in the filter at line 242.
tasks.executor_notes: Unused-track counting now ignores the default V1/A1 tracks and clear-unused preserves them explicitly before considering fallback empty-track retention. This matches the empty-default-track edge case the plan called out.

tasks.description: EditorPane overlay behavior (bug #8). SETTLED DECISION: overlay when open/hovering, push only when locked (matching GenerationsPane pattern). File: src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.tsx:40. Change line 40 from `paddingTop: isVideoEditorShellActive ? '0px' : ((isEditorPaneLocked || isEditorPaneOpen) ? \`${effectiveEditorPaneHeight}px\` : '0px')` to `paddingTop: isVideoEditorShellActive ? '0px' : (isEditorPaneLocked ? \`${effectiveEditorPaneHeight}px\` : '0px')`. This removes the `isEditorPaneOpen` condition so the pane only pushes content when locked, not when hovering/open. EditorPaneTab.tsx:121-134 already uses `fixed top-0` with zIndex 59, so overlay behavior is already correct for the pane surface itself.
tasks.executor_notes: LayoutMainContent now only adds top padding when the editor pane is locked, so hover-open behaves as an overlay while locked mode still reserves space. Added a focused regression test for the hover-open case.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/components/ui/slider.test.tsx src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts

tasks.description: Fix text and image overlay drag stickiness (bugs #4/#5). SETTLED DECISION: use local state offset only during drag, commit on mouseUp. Do NOT call onOverlayChange during mousemove. File: src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx. Current behavior: dragOverride state (line 40) updates the bounding box position in effectiveOverlays (line 127-137), but the Remotion-rendered content inside stays at the committed position until mouseUp. The bounding box overlay div DOES move (line 296-301 converts bounds to CSS), but the actual text/image content rendered by Remotion doesn't update because onOverlayChange only fires in commitDragChange (line 182). FIX: The overlay bounding box already moves correctly via dragOverride — the 'stickiness' the user sees is the Remotion content underneath not matching. Since we must NOT call onOverlayChange on every mousemove (perf risk), instead: during drag, HIDE the Remotion-rendered content for the dragged clip and show a lightweight CSS-only preview in the overlay div. Add a `isDragging` derived state, and when dragging a text clip, render the text content directly in the overlay div (using clipMeta.text.content + styles). For image clips, show the asset thumbnail or a placeholder. This gives visual feedback without touching the Remotion pipeline. Implementation: (1) Derive `const draggingId = dragOverride?.actionId ?? null;` (2) In the overlay render loop (line 306-362), when `overlay.actionId === draggingId`, render a lightweight preview (text content for text clips, asset URL for images) INSTEAD of just the bounding box frame. The overlay already has the correct CSS position from effectiveOverlays.
tasks.executor_notes: Kept drag updates local to OverlayEditor: the dragged overlay now renders its own lightweight preview while an opaque mask covers the original Remotion position, so text and image overlays visually follow the cursor without re-materializing the timeline on every mousemove.

tasks.description: Condense timeline controls (bug #6). File: src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:151-174. APPROACH: Remove the separate toolbar div (lines 152-174) and integrate controls into the divider row. Change the divider (lines 142-149) from a plain drag handle into a compact toolbar strip that contains: add-track buttons (V/A), 'Clear N unused' link, zoom in/out buttons, AND the drag handle. Make the strip ~28px tall (up from 10px). Layout: flex row with controls on left, drag handle centered, zoom on right. Keep the onDividerMouseDown handler on the strip's central drag handle element. This eliminates the ~32px toolbar row and uses the divider space. Ensure the drag-resize still works by keeping the mousedown handler on the handle portion only.
tasks.executor_notes: Merged track management, clear-unused, zoom, and the resize affordance into the divider strip so the standalone toolbar row is gone. The resize handler stays attached only to the center handle, which preserves divider dragging while buttons remain clickable.

tasks.description: Preview resize / maximize timeline toggle (bug #1). File: src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx. Add a 'maximize timeline' toggle button. (1) Add state: `const [isTimelineMaximized, setIsTimelineMaximized] = useState(false);` (2) Add a Maximize2/Minimize2 icon button in the toolbar strip (from T6) or the divider area. (3) When active, override gridTemplateRows to `${MIN_PREVIEW_HEIGHT}px auto 1fr` (preview at minimum 180px, timeline takes rest). (4) When deactivated, revert to the existing timelineHeight-based or default split. (5) The divider drag should auto-deactivate the maximize toggle (set false on mouseUp in onDividerMouseDown). Modify the style computation at line 115: `gridTemplateRows: isTimelineMaximized ? \`${MIN_PREVIEW_HEIGHT}px auto 1fr\` : (timelineHeight ? \`minmax(0,1fr) auto ${timelineHeight}px\` : 'minmax(0,1fr) auto minmax(200px,36%)')`.
tasks.executor_notes: Added a dedicated maximize toggle that snaps the preview to the configured minimum height and restores the prior split when toggled off. Manual divider dragging automatically exits maximize mode, so the two layout controls do not fight each other.

tasks.description: Render button: show 'not available yet' toast properly (bug #2). SETTLED DECISION: Do NOT attempt canvas capture or client-side render. Show clear user-facing message. File: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts:603-606. Change `toast.error('Render is not available yet')` to `toast.info('Render is not available yet — server-side rendering coming soon.')`. Change from error to info since it's a known limitation, not an error. Keep the existing startRender infrastructure (setRenderProgress, renderStatus) for future server-side implementation.
tasks.executor_notes: Left rendering disabled per the approved plan and changed the user feedback from an error toast to an informational message that explicitly frames the limitation as pending server-side work.

tasks.description: Timeline clip drag visual feedback (bug #3). Files: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useCrossTrackDrag.ts, src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/timeline-overrides.css. Cross-track drag already has a ghost element (.cross-track-ghost with opacity 0.8, z-index 99999). The issue is likely WITHIN-track horizontal dragging (handled by @xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor library natively). Investigate: (1) Check if the library applies any CSS class during inline drag. (2) Look at getActionRender callback in TimelineEditor.tsx — this renders custom clip visuals. (3) Add CSS for drag state: `.timeline-wrapper .timeline-editor-action:active { opacity: 0.7; }` as a starting point. (4) For the drop position indicator during cross-track drag, the ghost element already exists. (5) Check if the library supports onActionMoving callback (it does per grep results in TimelineEditor.tsx and useTimelineState.ts) — use this to show a time indicator at the target position. (6) Add a vertical drop-position line CSS: `.timeline-wrapper .drop-position-line { position: absolute; width: 2px; background: #38bdf8; z-index: 100; }` and render it during drag via the onActionMoving callback.
tasks.executor_notes: Added explicit timeline drag feedback for horizontal clip moves: the dragged clip now gets a brighter active state plus a projected ghost, center line, and row label that track the target slot. Existing cross-track ghost/highlight behavior remains intact.

tasks.description: Debug and fix external image drag-drop (bug #10). File: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts:573-644. The drop handler already exists with skeleton clips + uploadAsset. Debug approach: (1) Add console.log('[drop] files:', files.length) at line 579 to verify the handler fires. (2) Check that onTimelineDragOver calls event.preventDefault() — this is REQUIRED for drops to work. Search for the dragOver handler. (3) The timeline wrapper at TimelineEditor.tsx likely attaches onDrop/onDragOver — verify these props are connected. (4) Check if the @xzdarcy timeline library intercepts and swallows the drop event before our handler runs. If so, attach the drop listener to a parent wrapper div above the library's DOM. (5) Test with a simple image file from Finder. (6) If uploadAsset fails, check the upload function and Supabase Storage bucket permissions.
tasks.executor_notes: Reused the existing skeleton placeholder flow but changed dropped external images to follow the generation pipeline: upload the image to the normal media storage path, create a generation record plus primary variant, register that generation as a timeline asset, then add the clip. Non-image files keep the existing timeline-bucket upload path.

tasks.description: Verify assets panel defaults to closed (bug #11). SETTLED DECISION: skip if already done. File: src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/PropertiesPanel.tsx:25. VERIFIED: `const [assetsExpanded, setAssetsExpanded] = useState(false);` — assets panel already defaults to collapsed. No code change needed. Mark as done after confirming in browser if possible.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified in code that PropertiesPanel already initializes assetsExpanded to false, so no repo changes were needed for this item.

tasks.description: Investigate and fix duplicate queries warning (bug #14). SETTLED DECISION: EditorPaneTab already returns null on editor route (line 67-69), so coexisting providers are NOT the cause. Investigate: (1) Check if React strict mode double-mount causes duplicate subscriptions — look for StrictMode in main.tsx or App.tsx. In dev mode, React 18+ double-mounts components. This would cause temporary duplicate queries that resolve on unmount. If strict mode: this warning is benign in dev only, add a comment. (2) Check if useTimelineData and useTimelinesList share overlapping query keys — useTimelineData uses ['timeline', id] and useTimelinesList uses a separate key, so no overlap. (3) Check if useTimelineRealtime also creates queries with the same key — it only invalidates, doesn't create new queries. (4) Most likely cause: React strict mode double-mount in development. If confirmed, no fix needed — document that it's a dev-only artifact. (5) Alternative: if NOT strict mode, add `staleTime: 5000` to the timeline query (useTimelineData.ts:190) to prevent immediate re-fetch on re-mount.
tasks.executor_notes: The duplicate-query warning was not caused by coexisting editor providers. The actual issue was AssetPanel building useQueries entries with repeated generation IDs, which TanStack warns about. Deduplicating the query list removes the warning while still mapping each asset back to its shared query result.

tasks.description: Build and test verification. Run `npm run build` to verify no TypeScript errors. Run `npm test` for test suites touching modified files. Manual verification checklist: compact preview slider clickable/draggable, no Remotion license warning, 'Clear N unused' excludes V1/A1, editor pane overlays not pushes when open, overlay drag shows visual feedback, timeline controls condensed, maximize timeline toggle works, render button shows info toast, clip drag has visual feedback, external file drop works, no duplicate query warnings in dev console.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran a full production build and targeted regression tests for the touched shared components. I did not run the repository-wide npm test suite, so verification covers the build plus focused tests rather than every existing test file.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/components/ui/slider.test.tsx src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts

watch_items: PERF: T5 overlay drag — do NOT call onOverlayChange during mousemove. The pipeline (applyTimelineEdit → materializeData → rowsToConfig → setState → Remotion re-render) is too heavy for 60fps updates. Use local CSS-only preview during drag, commit on mouseUp only.
RENDER: T8 — do NOT attempt canvas capture or MediaRecorder. Remotion Player renders DOM, not canvas. captureStream() won't work. Just show info toast.

SLIDER: T1 — base-ui Slider onValueChange type depends on whether value prop is scalar or array. Since CompactPreview passes value={[currentTime]} (array), base-ui sends number[]. The wrapper (slider.tsx:31) passes it through without unwrapping. Verify actual runtime behavior before committing fix direction.

DUPLICATE QUERIES: T12 — EditorPaneTab already gates on isVideoEditorRoute (line 67-69), returning null on the editor page. Coexisting providers are impossible. Most likely React strict mode double-mount in dev.
CONDENSE CONTROLS: T6 — when merging toolbar into divider, ensure the drag-resize mousedown handler only fires on the central handle element, not on the buttons. stopPropagation on button clicks.

OVERLAY BEHAVIOR: T4 — GenerationsPane also pushes on paddingBottom when locked OR open. EditorPane change only removes isEditorPaneOpen from the push condition, keeping push for isEditorPaneLocked. Verify this matches user intent.

CROSS-TRACK DRAG: T9 — the ghost element (.cross-track-ghost) already exists for vertical drag. The missing feedback may be for horizontal within-track repositioning handled by the library. Investigate which scenario lacks feedback before implementing.

FILE DROP: T10 — the handler code exists and looks complete. The bug is likely an event propagation issue (timeline library swallowing drop events) or a missing event.preventDefault() in dragOver. Debug before writing new code.
ASSETS PANEL: T11 — already verified as done. assetsExpanded defaults to false at PropertiesPanel.tsx:25. Skip unless browser testing reveals otherwise.

sense_checks.question: After fix, does the compact preview playhead slider respond to both click and drag without console errors?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified by normalizing Slider to emit a scalar value and updating CompactPreview to consume that scalar. Build passed and the shared slider tests still pass.

sense_checks.question: Is the Remotion license console warning suppressed after adding the prop? Verify the prop name matches the current @remotion/player version.
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed from node_modules/@remotion/player/dist/cjs/Player.d.ts that acknowledgeRemotionLicense is a valid prop, then wired it into RemotionPreview.

sense_checks.question: With an empty V1 and A1 (no clips), does the 'Clear N unused' button show count 0? With V1, A1, and an empty V2, does it show count 1?
sense_checks.executor_note: The counting logic now excludes DEFAULT_VIDEO_TRACKS and clear-unused keeps those IDs unconditionally, so empty V1/A1 no longer contribute to the count.

sense_checks.question: When the editor pane is open (hovering, not locked), does the page content underneath remain at its original position (no padding-top shift)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Added a focused LayoutMainContent test covering the hover-open editor pane case and it now keeps paddingTop at 0px unless the pane is locked.

sense_checks.question: During drag of a text overlay, does the text content visually follow the mouse? Does the final drop commit the correct position? No lag during drag?
sense_checks.executor_note: Overlay drag remains local-state-only. The dragged overlay now paints its own preview while a mask covers the stale Remotion position, which resolves the visual stickiness without per-mousemove timeline commits.

sense_checks.question: Are the track management and zoom controls now integrated into the divider area with no separate toolbar row? Does the divider drag-resize still work correctly?
sense_checks.executor_note: Controls now live in the divider strip and the resize handler is bound only to the center handle element, so button clicks do not trigger panel resizing.

sense_checks.question: Does clicking the maximize toggle snap the preview to minimum height and give the timeline maximum space? Does clicking again restore the original split?
sense_checks.executor_note: The maximize toggle switches gridTemplateRows to a minimum-height preview layout and toggling off falls back to the prior split logic; divider drags clear maximize mode.

sense_checks.question: Does clicking the Render button show an info toast (not error) saying rendering is not available yet?
sense_checks.executor_note: startRender now raises an informational toast message instead of an error and keeps the render infrastructure dormant for future server-side work.

sense_checks.question: When dragging a clip on the timeline (both horizontal and cross-track), is there clear visual feedback showing the target position?
sense_checks.executor_note: TimelineEditor now renders an explicit ghost, center line, and row label during horizontal clip drags; cross-track drag feedback continues to come from the existing ghost/highlight path.

sense_checks.question: When dragging an image file from Finder onto the timeline, does it upload to Supabase Storage and appear as a clip? Does the skeleton placeholder show during upload?
sense_checks.executor_note: Dropped external images now follow a generation-backed upload flow before being added to the timeline, while the existing uploading-* skeleton placeholder remains in place until the async work resolves.

sense_checks.question: Is the assets panel in PropertiesPanel collapsed on first render? (Already verified in code — assetsExpanded defaults to false.)
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified existing code only: PropertiesPanel initializes assetsExpanded with useState(false).

sense_checks.question: After investigation, is the duplicate queries warning explained and either fixed or documented as a dev-only strict mode artifact?
sense_checks.executor_note: Investigated the warning path and fixed the real cause by deduplicating repeated generation-lightbox queries in AssetPanel rather than changing provider mounting behavior.

sense_checks.question: Does `npm run build` pass with zero TypeScript errors? Do all existing tests pass?
sense_checks.executor_note: npm run build passed and the targeted vitest run passed. The full repository-wide npm test suite was not run in this execution pass.

meta_commentary:
EXECUTION ORDER: T1-T4 + T8 + T11 + T12 are independent — execute in parallel or any order. T5 (overlay drag) is independent but complex. T6 depends on T3 (uses unusedTrackCount). T7 depends on T6 (shares the divider UI). T9 and T10 are independent investigation tasks. T13 is final verification.

CRITICAL OVERRIDES FROM REVISION GUIDANCE:
1. T5: Do NOT call onOverlayChange during mousemove. Use local CSS preview only. The original plan's suggestion to call onOverlayChange on every move is explicitly overridden.
2. T8: Do NOT attempt canvas capture or any client-side render approach. Just improve the toast message.
3. T11: Already done (verified: assetsExpanded defaults to false). Skip code changes.
4. T12: Don't assume coexisting providers. Investigate strict mode and shared query keys.

KEY FILE LOCATIONS:
- Slider wrapper: src/shared/components/ui/slider.tsx (line 31 — passthrough bug)
- CompactPreview: src/tools/video-editor/components/CompactPreview.tsx (line 80)
- RemotionPreview: src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.tsx (line 95-109)
- Track management: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts (lines 224-261)
- Default tracks: src/tools/video-editor/lib/defaults.ts (V1, A1)
- Layout padding: src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.tsx (line 40)
- Overlay drag: src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx (lines 127-137, 182-241)
- Shell layout: src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx (lines 61-97, 142-178)
- Render stub: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts (lines 603-606)
- Cross-track drag: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useCrossTrackDrag.ts
- Timeline CSS: src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/timeline-overrides.css
- File drop: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts (lines 573-644)
- Properties panel: src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/PropertiesPanel.tsx (line 25)
- Editor pane: src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx (lines 67-69, 121-134)

RISK: T5 (overlay drag) and T6/T7 (condense+maximize) are the most complex tasks. T5 requires rendering clip content in the overlay div during drag — need access to clipMeta for text content/styling and asset URLs for images. T6 changes the divider from a simple handle to a toolbar strip, which could break the drag-resize if mousedown propagation isn't handled carefully.
